text: After introduction of the working groups report, Brazil, Mera motion, calling for two independent votes, and that was to be followed by a consideration of every P3 power got 32, 11, 32, 12. This, after the Brazilian's motion, Singapore, may have consideration that the vote be deferred. Singapore, consideration that the consider of different votes was followed by Saudi Arabia, asking for a row of votes on the deferred of the vote as Singapore has proposed. At this point, Denmark made a point of order on the discussion will be fed to the Brazilian proposal, and proposed that the Brazilian proposal be considered. Right after that, we started receiving marries of emotions that were actually premature. So my appeal is, let us consider the vote be deferred, and then, when we come to the WP3, we cannot start taking all the veil of emotions and add them to our relevant. So I am appealing to you, let us conclude, there will be four, after that we will come to the WP3, because that's where most of these emotions are actually relevant. Let us be my appeal to you. If that is okay, we can break for coffee, and then, when we return, we can start the consideration. If you agree with me, please lower your cards. I am seeing Saudi and Brazil. I know the appeal. I am trying to see how we can have some structure order and discussions, because I know there will be many emotions. You are arguing what are these emotions as to do with the adoption of ESO, with the WP3. That's the number of appeal. Continuing the consideration of the WP3, when we come to WP3, you will have all the freedoms to table your mirror emotions. Thank you. All right, I see Saudi and Brazil stay holding the floor. Saudi Arabia. Thank you very much, Chair. Allow us to sympathize with you, the Secretary General, the Secretary of our fellow members, and express our lack of desire to be engaging in this. I don't know what to call it, to be honest. But we are just here to say that we agree with many of the points that you referenced, or would like to add a couple of clarifiers from our side. When Denmark raised a point of order in relation to our motion, the Chair presented the legal advisor and presented a ruling on the matter that's procedurally correct. No one appealed this ruling, because as per the rules of procedure on points of orders, if there is a appeal, it would be immediately put to a vote, but no appeal was done. This is considered a decision of this committee. Then we move on. What should have happened was our motion should have allowed for ourselves and an additional supporter to our motion to speak on the matter, to opponents, to our motion to speak on the matter, after which the matter should have been immediately put to a vote, by a rule called as per the rules of procedure, the to our request. So while we understand, Chair, that your proposal is to finish on WP4, then move on to WP3. What the rules say we have to do is address the motion that we have. It is our understanding that one delegation took the floor in relation to our ruling and called an in opposition to our ruling at this moment called for another motion of suspension. In light of what's going on here in this room, we think a suspension would be wise. After the suspension, we would have to revert back and ask for the rules immediately deal with our motion. The question that we put to vote requires a German and therefore the legal advice presented by the Secretariat is correct in our view. Once we deal with the motion of a German, in line with the question that we presented to the committee, if the motion succeeds, this is it, we adjourn. If it doesn't succeed, then we may consider further actions as appropriate. Thank you. Saudi Arabia, if you follow the structure I just gave in the sequence of things, I see a quite correct, even quite be aware, let me say, one of our takes precedence and then Max Panapola was to ensure that Brazil is a proposal that we consider WP4 and finish, and then move on to WP3 for all these things to be considered. So that was then my point of order. And then right after that, that's when we started having all these motions that prematurely disrupted things, I didn't want to only then Max Panapola. So I'll give the floor to Brazil and then like Brad Dunn will break has my advice and then we'll come back, we'll follow, we'll take WP4 and then we'll come to WP2 with looking to all matters. Brazil, please. Thank you chair and let me say I agree with my friend from Saudi, we also sympathize with you and the Secretary it. We understand that the rules are being quite significant to we test, but we understand that they are designed to guide us through moments exactly like this. That's why they exist. Now in practice, a suspension is already happening. Right, again we're playing the game of time that we mentioned before. I think it's important that we notice that delaying further the decision is not a neutral stance. And that is why we try to offer constructive way forward. And the way involved accepting by consensus as a group, language and the cover of a solution and then voting. Now many delegations have asked for actions to be taken after that. One of them, specifically the point of order raised by Denmark, under the rules of procedure requiring immediate action, it seems to be very clear that no further actions can be taken before the point of order is resolved. So we understand that it's difficult to go back to business as usual ignoring that fact. We would then very kindly ask you to please take immediate action about that point of order. Thank you. Thank you, Brazil. Aberia. Thank you, chair. Before I begin my statement, chair, I just want to express our concern with respect to the fact that we had a flow before the motion. Unfortunately, we were not given the flow after that motion was heard. Thank you very much. Chair, we believe the intervention made by Singapore is profoundly significant. I think Angus, the spirit of unity, which is critical to upholding the togetherness of the IMO. Liberia as one of the largest flag states, re-eccuses the concerns raised by Singapore regarding the deferment of this vote, to make more room for more consultations for a consensus vote to retain the unity of the IMO. As we all can see, this room is immensely tense and divided. Hence, if care or leadership is not considered, we all will leave London with a fragmented IMO. We do see merit in the proposal calling for unity over division and do agree that voting or taking any critical decision at this stage could potentially bring about more divisions which will severely honor my IMO spirit of unity. Hence, we also align with Saudi Arabia. Thank you, Mr. Chair. I think we will be able to, as I said, the part of the sequence of things I gave them and point of order was the first basis that Brazil has listed at that point of order. So, looking at the time, I cannot keep the temperatures after going more than 10 minutes or time waiting. When I come back, I don't wear a braver coffee now. Wear a wool on this part of order and then we'll see tickets out from further. I'm going to have a drink. Nigeria, I want to tell you what, have fun in Nigeria.
